---
title: My dynamic report 
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}
body {
  background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #FADBFF, #d68dcf);
  font-family: Cascadia Code SemiBold;
  color: #2B3022;
  text-align: center;
}

h1 {
  color: #2B3022;
}

p {
  color: #397F3E;
}

a {
  font-size: 20px;
  color: #4C9453;
}
```


# ✩ The music-listening habits of myself and close friends ✩

## For this dynamic report, I created a google form survey and shared it with myself and close friends. The survey collected data on what music genre their listening session consisted of, as well as the location, time and duration of the session. I aim to use this data to put into perspective our listening habits!


```{r}
library(tidyverse)

logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vSk4d7ILYbLKLZ3vgs0IcStMdLZwUR6fsE-MtcTA7ivy1qcAG4kBRviq5E3eIcIgVcAmSb3kfHyQ4-e/pub?output=csv")

# Renaming
latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    Time = `What was the approximate time your listening session started?`,
    Genre = `What genres did you listen to?`,
    Platform = `What platform did you use to listen to your music?`,
    Duration = `Approximately how long (minutes) was your listening session?`,
    Location = `Where did the listening session happen?`,
    Mood = `How do you notice your mood change after the listening session?`
  )

# Code I have decided to use --------------------------------------------------------------------------
```
# 𝄞 My Graphs 𝄞
### ♪ Graph 1 - How much time was spent listening to each genre? ♪

```{r}
#Duration x Genre - Graph
# Separate genres 
genre_data <- latest_data %>%
  separate_rows(Genre, sep = ", ")

# Summarise total listening time by genre
genre_summary <- genre_data %>%
  group_by(Genre) %>%
  summarise(Total_Time = sum(Duration, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Time))

ggplot(genre_summary, aes(x = reorder(Genre, -Total_Time), y = Total_Time)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#ffcfec") +
  labs(
    title = "Total Listening Time by Genre",
    subtitle = "Looking at which music genres were listened to for the longest",
    caption = "Source: Data collection survey",
    x = "Genre",
    y = "Total Listening Time (minutes)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

### ♬ Graph 2 - What time is music listened to the most? ♬

```{r}
# Duration x Time of Day - Graph

# Get hours
time_data <- latest_data %>%
  mutate(Hour = hour(hms(Time)))

# Plot 
ggplot(time_data, aes(x = Hour)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#8fb58a") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 23, by = 5),
    labels = c("12am", "5am", "10am", "3pm", "8pm")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Listening Activity by Time of Day",
    subtitle = "What time did people listen to music the most?",
    caption = "Source: Data collection survey",
    x = "Time of Day",
    y = "Number of Listening Sessions"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

hour_summary <- time_data %>%
  count(Hour, sort = TRUE)

peak_hour <- hour_summary$Hour[1]
peak_count <- hour_summary$n[1]

# Making AM and PM ;-;
formatted_time <- ifelse(
  peak_hour == 0, "12am",
  ifelse(peak_hour == 12, "12pm",
         ifelse(peak_hour < 12,
                paste0(peak_hour, "am"),
                paste0(peak_hour - 12, "pm")))
)

cat(paste("The peak listening time is", formatted_time,
          "with", peak_count, "listening sessions recorded at that time."))
```

# ♩ My Summary Values ♩
```{r}
# mean time
mean_duration <- round(mean(latest_data$Duration, na.rm = TRUE), 1)

cat(paste("The average listening duration is", mean_duration, "minutes."))


# max time
max_duration <- max(latest_data$Duration, na.rm = TRUE)

cat(paste("The longest listening session lasted", max_duration, "minutes."))

genre_counts <- genre_data %>%
  count(Genre, sort = TRUE)

# most picked genre
top_genre <- genre_counts$Genre[1]
top_count <- genre_counts$n[1]

cat(paste("The most frequently listened to genre is", top_genre,
          "with", top_count, "occurrences."))

# fav platform
platform_counts <- latest_data %>%
  count(Platform, sort = TRUE)

top_platform <- platform_counts$Platform[1]
top_platform_count <- platform_counts$n[1]

total_users <- sum(platform_counts$n)

cat(paste("The most popular platform is", top_platform,
          "with", top_platform_count, "out of", total_users,
          "users selecting it."))

```

# At the time of publishing this report (4/1/2026), it seems that rock is the most listened to genre!
# ROCK ON!!!
![](https://media.tenor.com/5CKuXIOwRhIAAAAi/%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%BA.gif)
